goal: Resolve the remaining full-suite failures caused by legacy tests and CLI paths still assuming the pre-v0.3.11 heavy default governance layout, while preserving the v0.3.11 lean default model.

approval_evidence.summary: Human asked the current session to optimize compressed context and continue after being told the next step was a second compatibility/test-migration round.

inputs: Full unittest failure snapshot from PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
scope_in: Update legacy compatibility code and tests needed to make the current full unittest suite coherent with the v0.3.11 lean default.
Preserve lean default behavior: do not recreate .governance/changes, .governance/archive, .governance/runtime, .governance/index, or .governance/local as normal v0.3.11 runtime output.
Keep changes focused on the observed failing tests and any directly shared helper they exercise.
Maintain a low-context recovery summary after each substantial phase.
scope_out: Reverting unrelated user edits in .governance/AGENTS.md or .governance/agent-playbook.md.
Restoring the legacy heavy layout as the default v0.3.11 behavior.
Changing release metadata, tagging, pushing, or publishing.
Reading the original failed session JSONL.
acceptance: Each compatibility change is backed by a failing or already-failing test observation.
Targeted v0.3.11 lean tests continue to pass.
The previously failing legacy tests pass or are explicitly migrated with narrow assertions.
A fresh full unittest run is performed and reported with exact result.
expected_outputs: Passing targeted compatibility tests for the prior 2 failures and 4 errors.
